title: OR degree indices and adjacent moves
summary: Encodes triples (a,b,c) with a+b+c=N for the words Δ̃ᵃ((Δ̃ᵇf)(Δ̃ᶜg)), constructs their finite support, and defines degree-preserving adjacent moves.

mathematicalContent.heading: Degree-N indices
mathematicalContent.body: An OR index is a nonnegative triple whose sum is N. It represents one rank-two ambient Laplacian monomial.

mathematicalContent.heading: Finite support
mathematicalContent.body: The canonical support enumerates all triples with entries between 0 and N and filters by a+b+c=N.

mathematicalContent.heading: Adjacent moves
mathematicalContent.body: Six moves transfer one unit between two coordinates while preserving total degree; the required source coordinate must be positive.

correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Stores a nonnegative triple (a,b,c) of total degree N.

correspondence.leanType: def toWord {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) : LaplacianWord 2
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Associates the rank-two Laplacian word with outer power a and input powers b,c.

correspondence.leanType: theorem toWord_totalPower {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) : I.toWord.totalPower = N
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Proves that the word associated with a degree-N index has total Laplacian degree N.

correspondence.leanType: def supportRaw (N : ℕ) : Finset ((ℕ × ℕ) × ℕ)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Builds a raw finite set of bounded triples and filters by the degree equation.
Packages every raw triple with its proof of a+b+c=N to form the canonical OR support.

correspondence.leanType: theorem support_toWord_totalPower {N : ℕ} {I : ORIndex N} (_hI : I ∈ support N) : I.toWord.totalPower = N
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Shows that every supported index represents a word of degree N.

correspondence.leanType: def move_a_to_b {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (ha : 0 < I.a) : ORIndex N
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Moves one unit from the outer exponent a to the first input exponent b.

correspondence.leanType: def move_a_to_c {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (ha : 0 < I.a) : ORIndex N
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Moves one unit from the outer exponent a to the second input exponent c.

correspondence.leanType: def move_b_to_a {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (hb : 0 < I.b) : ORIndex N
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Moves one unit from the first input exponent b to the outer exponent a.

correspondence.leanType: def move_c_to_a {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (hc : 0 < I.c) : ORIndex N
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Moves one unit from the second input exponent c to the outer exponent a.

correspondence.leanType: def move_b_to_c {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (hb : 0 < I.b) : ORIndex N
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Moves one unit from the first input exponent b to the second input exponent c.

correspondence.leanType: def move_c_to_b {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (hc : 0 < I.c) : ORIndex N
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Moves one unit from the second input exponent c to the first input exponent b.

correspondence.leanType: theorem N_sub_b_pos_of_a_pos {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (ha : 0 < I.a) : 0 < N - I.b
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: If a>0, proves that N−b is positive.

correspondence.leanType: theorem N_sub_c_pos_of_a_pos {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (ha : 0 < I.a) : 0 < N - I.c
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: If a>0, proves that N−c is positive.

correspondence.leanType: theorem N_sub_b_pred_eq {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (_ha : 0 < I.a) : N - I.b - 1 = N - (I.b + 1)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Reassociates the predecessor of N−b for the a-to-b move.

correspondence.leanType: theorem N_sub_c_pred_eq {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) (_ha : 0 < I.a) : N - I.c - 1 = N - (I.c + 1)
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Reassociates the predecessor of N−c for the a-to-c move.

correspondence.leanType: theorem b_succ_add_c {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) : I.b + 1 + I.c = I.b + I.c + 1
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Normalizes the sum after increasing b.

correspondence.leanType: theorem b_add_c_succ {N : ℕ} (I : ORIndex N) : I.b + (I.c + 1) = I.b + I.c + 1
correspondence.mathematicalMeaning: Normalizes the sum after increasing c.

references.locator: Classification of invariant bilinear differential operators
references.note: Flat-model origin of the coefficient family.
Primary curved ambient source for the encoded OR recurrence.

limitations: This is finite combinatorial indexing and arithmetic. It does not prove that support contains each index uniquely as an extensional theorem, nor any coefficient recurrence, defect formula, or tangentiality result.
